content:
Overall, this is a strong submission with a few areas that could benefit from added clarity and troubleshooting details. Great work!

Best,
[Your Name]

⸻

Juana Casallas Diaz – “How to Trim Your Dog’s Nails Safely”

Dear Juana,

Your instructions on trimming a dog’s nails are clear, easy to follow, and written with a friendly tone that makes the process seem approachable. I really appreciate how you included steps for keeping the dog calm, as well as a section on handling accidental bleeding. These are important considerations that add value to your guide.

One suggestion for improvement is to enhance the formatting of your steps. Numbering each step and using bolded titles would improve readability. Additionally, visuals—such as diagrams showing the correct way to hold the paw or where to clip—would be extremely beneficial, especially for new pet owners who might be nervous about hurting their dog.

Your conclusion is solid, but you might consider briefly mentioning the long-term benefits of regular nail trimming, such as improved posture and joint health for dogs. Overall, this is a fantastic guide that just needs some minor structural and visual enhancements to be even more effective.
context: From document: Workshop-Style Feedback Letters — chunk 5 (chunk 5/7)
